beats.text: Here's a vector with sixty-four coordinates — one intensity per pixel in an eight-by-eight image block.
Now swap the basis. The equation x equals W times c means the same image, totally different coordinates. The pixel grid fades and sixty-four frequency waves appear in its place.
Watch the coefficients c light up one by one. Most land near zero — only a handful carry real information.
Slash the small ones. Keep three out of sixty-four. The reconstructed block snaps back — almost identical to the original.
That's JPEG. Twenty-one-to-one compression, barely any visible loss. Change of basis isn't just math — it's how every image on your phone survives.
